anti_patterns.description: Controllers and models that handle too many responsibilities

issues: Handles importing domains from 3 external providers (Brevo, SparkPost, VPS)
locations.suggested_refactor: Split into domain service, verification service, import service, and keep controller thin

issues: Handles campaign launching with complex domain rotation logic
Handles tracking link injection and wrapping
Handles campaign deletion with related data cleanup
locations.suggested_refactor: Extract campaign launching logic to campaign service, recipient parsing to utility

issues: Performs complex MongoDB aggregation pipelines directly in controller
locations.suggested_refactor: Extract aggregation logic to analytics service or model methods
Split into auth context, layout components, and use route-based code splitting

anti_patterns.description: Functions that are too long and handle multiple responsibilities

locations.suggested_refactor: Split into parseLine, buildEventFromMatch, updateMessageIdMap, cleanupExpiredMappings

issues: Creates job array for each recipient
Updates campaign status and round-robin index
Has complex rollback logic in catch block
locations.suggested_refactor: Extract domain selection, job creation, campaign update, and error rollback to separate functions
Extract DKIM generation and DNS record building to domain service
Extract database connection, repair logic, parser initialization, queue setup, event handling, and shutdown to separate functions

anti_patterns.description: Code with excessive nesting levels making it hard to read

issues: Deep object creation with multiple conditions
locations.suggested_refactor: Extract network error detection to helper function, use early returns
Use map with Promise.all for parallel validation

anti_patterns.description: Hard-coded numbers and strings that should be constants

locations.suggested_refactor: Move to configuration or constants file
Already well-encapsulated in model, but could be configurable
Make configurable via options or environment variables
Extract to configuration constants

locations.magic_value: 50 (limit for recent events), 100 (limit for failed events)
locations.suggested_refactor: Make configurable via parameters or constants
Make configurable

anti_patterns.description: Inconsistent naming conventions across the codebase

locations.issue: Backend uses camelCase for functions/variables, frontend uses camelCase for components but mixed for hooks/vars
locations.suggested_refactor: Establish and enforce consistent naming conventions

locations.issue: Some routes use plural nouns (/domains), some use singular with plural in path (/seeds/:id/toggle)
locations.suggested_refactor: Establish RESTful naming conventions

anti_patterns.description: Synchronous operations in asynchronous contexts that could block the event loop

locations.issue: DNS lookups and cryptographic operations could be slow
locations.context: Actually looks like it's just parsing, but the injectEvent method does have await
locations.suggested_refactor: Already using async/await properly

locations.issue: DNS resolution functions are async but called sequentially
locations.context: verifyDomain function calls checkMX, checkSPF, checkDKIM, checkDMARC sequentially
locations.suggested_refactor: Already using Promise.all - good!

summary: The codebase shows several anti-patterns, primarily God objects (domainController, campaignController, App.jsx) and long methods (launchCampaign, _buildEvent, startServer). These represent significant refactoring opportunities to improve maintainability and testability.
